result:
The squash commit `d6ea1e3` is on remote `main` with all 17 application files. Delivery is complete.

## Delivery summary — Story #9 landed ✅

**`/deliver 9 --yes`** discovered a single `type::story` (v2 is Story-only; no Epic issue exists) and drove it end-to-end through the one delivery engine.

**Result:** PR [#10](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-21-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-54c0f9fe/pull/10) squash-merged to `main` as `d6ea1e3`; Issue #9 CLOSED at `agent::done`. Terminal status: **`landed`**.

### What shipped
The sandbox baseline carried no application source (only the framework overlay), so — exactly as the Story's `## Spec` directed for a genuinely-absent surface — I built the described API to the contract, dependency-free (Node built-in `node:sqlite`, `node:test`, `node:http`):

- **`src/lib/pagination.js`** — the single shared pagination helper. `DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE = 25` is the one constant every list endpoint reads (**AC-4**).
- **`src/reports/receivables.js`** — `GET /reports/receivables` paginated on the shared `page`/`pageSize` → `items`/`total`/`page`/`pageSize` envelope (**AC-1/AC-2**), with `totalOutstandingCents` as a dedicated `SUM` over the full filtered set — invariant across pages, never a page subtotal (**AC-3**).
- **`src/orders/list.js`, `src/customers/list.js`** — peer paginated endpoints proving the default is API-wide.
- **`db/migrations/20260721_add_receivables_index.sql`** — a partial index matching the receivables filter, serving both the page query and the grand-total aggregate (**AC-5**; query plan verified to use it, 6,000-order report responds in ~17ms, far under the 2s budget).
- **4 test files (12 tests)** covering AC-1…AC-5, all green.

### Gates
`typecheck`, `lint`, `test` (12/12), and `format` all passed; `check-baselines` self-skipped (greenfield); code-review and base-sync passed.

### Two environment quirks handled (logged as friction)
1. `.agents/` isn't committed in this sandbox, so it was absent from the worktree and the `check-baselines` gate's script couldn't resolve — fixed with a local (uncommitted) symlink to the main checkout's `.agents`.
2. `package.json` is on the harness's `.git/info/exclude` "framework overlay (never commit)" list, so it's intentionally not part of the delivered commit. The application is self-sufficient on Node built-ins, and `tsconfig.json`/`biome.json` (which *are* committed) tune the overlay's tooling to the code.

The final "arm-failure" block was a false negative (the local branch-delete step failed *after* the PR had already merged); the idempotent `single-story-confirm-merge.js` reconciled the stale label and ran the land tail.
